title: READ_TRACKER is process-global Mutex with single contention point across all file ops
description:
## Severity
Major

## Dimension
Concurrency / SOLID-D (DIP) / Decoupling

## Location
src/tools/file/mod.rs:26-72 (`READ_TRACKER` static + `ReadFileTracker` impl)

## Violation
```rust
pub static READ_TRACKER: std::sync::LazyLock<ReadFileTracker> =
    std::sync::LazyLock::new(ReadFileTracker::new);

pub struct ReadFileTracker {
    read_files: Mutex<Vec<PathBuf>>,
}
```

This is hardcoded global state. Every read, every edit, every notebook_edit takes the same Mutex (mark_read uses `lock()`; has_been_read also takes the lock). Under concurrent sessions (the multi-session todo design hints at the intent), all file tools serialize through this single lock. Worse:

1. The `read_files: Vec<PathBuf>` is O(N) per `contains()` check (line 63: `files.contains(&check_path)`). With the 10_000 cap, every edit performs up to 10k path comparisons under the lock.
2. The tracker is shared across all sessions — session A reading `/x` lets session B edit `/x` without reading it. Violates session isolation that `todo.rs` already implements via `CURRENT_SESSION_ID` thread-local.
3. `clear()` is global — a "new session" clear nukes every other session's tracker.

DIP violation: `execute_edit_file` reaches directly into the global static rather than depending on an injected `ReadTrackingPolicy` trait.

## Mandated refactor
1. Replace `Vec` with `HashSet<PathBuf>` (O(1) membership check; the LRU eviction comment is wrong — the current code does not implement LRU semantics on the read path, only insertion order).
2. Either drop the LRU pretense (use `HashSet`) or implement actual LRU using `linked_hash_map::LinkedHashMap`.
3. Bucket the tracker by session id (reuse the `SessionIdGuard` machinery already in `todo.rs`).
4. Define a `trait ReadTracker { fn mark(&self, p: &Path); fn was_read(&self, p: &Path) -> bool; }` and inject it into `execute_edit_file`/`execute_notebook_edit` so tests can substitute a fake.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
